text.title: growth-influencer-marketing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for growth-influencer-marketing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLMs hallucinate engagement rates and follower counts when asked to "check" a creator.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Models have no real-time data. Always pull live data via API (Modash, HypeAuditor) or scraping. Never trust a model's asserted metrics.

failure-mode.description: Agent-generated personalization produces generic patterns even when prompted for specificity ("I love your work on [TOPIC]").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require the agent to quote a specific post URL and a specific line from that post. Generic pattern outputs are worse than no outreach — they create negative brand impressions.

failure-mode.description: Skipping brand-safety filtering before approving a creator for outreach.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Scan last 30 post captions for political, NSFW, or controversy-adjacent terms before any outreach or content approval. One brand-safety incident can override a full quarter of positive campaign results.

failure-mode.description: Quoting USD-only rates to creators in India, Brazil, or the Philippines without local-currency equivalent.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mismatched expectations from currency confusion break deals. Agents should output both USD and local currency for any creator outside the US, EU, or UK.
